name: Claude Haiku 4.5
note: First Claude-4-generation Haiku, matching Sonnet 4 coding performance at one-third the cost and 2x+ the speed.

results.notes: SWE-bench Verified, averaged over 50 trials, 128K thinking budget; confirmed via search corroboration of the official announcement

independent evaluation; AA page is JS-rendered and could not be scraped directly via fetch tools, but the exact 64.6% figure was cross-confirmed via secondary aggregators (e.g. pricepertoken.com, morphllm.com) explicitly citing Artificial Analysis's Claude Haiku 4.5 page [Corrected during full-corpus audit: Directly fetched Anthropic's official Oct 15 2025 launch page and viewed its embedded benchmark-comparison chart image: Claude Haiku 4.5 scores 73.0% on GPQA Diamond (128K thinking budget, averaged over 10 runs per the page's own methodology footnotes). The existing corpus value (64.6%) was sourced only to a third-party aggregator (Artificial Analysis) whose page could not be directly scraped and ]
independent evaluation; same caveat as GPQA row — 80.0% figure cross-confirmed via secondary aggregators citing Artificial Analysis

Terminal-Bench, Terminus-2 agent framework, averaged over 11 runs (6 without thinking at 40.21%, 5 with 32K thinking budget at 41.75%); chart shows composite 41.0%. Confirmed via official launch-page comparison chart and methodology text.
AIME 2025, no-tools config (80.7%); the chart also shows a 96.3% 'with python tools' figure, but 80.7% is the fair apples-to-apples number matching how other Claude models in this corpus report AIME.
Multilingual MMLU, averaged over 10 runs across 14 non-English languages, 128K thinking budget.
Princeton HAL 'HAL Generalist Agent' scaffold, Claude Haiku 4.5 (Oct 2025 snapshot). GAIA validation set.

Vals AI independent eval, Thinking variant. LiveCodeBench 41.175%, from the model page's embedded score bars (raw HTML fetch). Not previously tracked (the corpus has 'terminal-bench', 'gpqa', 'mmlu-pro' etc. for this model but no coding-contest benchmark).

Vals AI independent eval, Thinking variant. 'Terminal-Bench 2.1' 43.82%, from the model page's embedded score data. Distinct from the corpus's existing 'terminal-bench' row (41.0, Anthropic's own Terminus-2-agent run of the original/easier Terminal-Bench suite) — this is the newer Terminal-Bench 2.x suite, which had no tracked result for this model.
False positive from an automated scanner misreading routine repeated-trial averaging language.
False positive: scanner matched the substring 'OSWorld' inside 'OSWorld-Verified' and mistook it for a second, separate benchmark reference.
False positive; the two domain scores merely appear in the same source chart, they are not blended together.
False positive on the conflation claim; score itself left as-is since it is the self-reported chart value and no primary-source contradiction was found.

SealedBench sealed capability index — composite of capability, token efficiency, speed and cost; rank 24/28 on the 2026-07-22 run (sealedbench.com). Listed as "Claude 4.5 Haiku" (Anthropic). Evaluated at the highest available thinking setting. SealedBench 'Claude 4.5 Haiku' is this corpus's 'Claude Haiku 4.5'.

verificationNotes:
Real model, confirmed via anthropic.com/news/claude-haiku-4-5 (release 2025-10-15, $1/$5 pricing, 200K context, 'one-third the cost and more than twice the speed of Sonnet 4' claim verified verbatim). IMPORTANT CORRECTION: input record marked status='deprecated', but Anthropic's official model-deprecations page (fetched 2026-07-12) lists claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 as 'Active' with tentative retirement 'not sooner than 2026-10-15' — this model is currently live, not deprecated. Status corrected to 'released' (closest enum to 'Active'); apiAvailable=true confirmed correct. GPQA/MMLU-Pro rows: could not directly scrape the JS-rendered artificialanalysis.ai page, but the specific numbers were independently corroborated by secondary sources quoting the same Artificial Analysis data, so kept (not dropped) with sourceUrl unchanged and caveat noted.

[2026-07-16] Adversarial re-check of 4 flagged COMPARABILITY findings (tau2-bench-telecom=83, tau2-bench-retail=83.2, tau2-bench-airline=63.6, osworld-verified=50.7): re-fetched the raw HTML of anthropic.com/news/claude-haiku-4-5 and confirmed its methodology footnotes ("τ2-bench: Scores were achieved averaging over 10 runs using extended thinking..."; "OSWorld-Verified framework..., averaged across 4 runs...") describe ordinary repeated-trial statistical averaging of a single benchmark/domain, not a composite blending multiple benchmarks. Cross-confirmed the individual figures against independent aggregators (llm-stats.com search snippets citing Telecom=83.0%/Retail=83.2% as separate values; Caylent's Oct-2025 Haiku 4.5 analysis independently citing the identical 50.7% OSWorld-Verified figure vs. Sonnet 4's 42.2%). Arithmetic check also rules out a 3-way average across the τ2-bench domains: (83+83.2+63.6)/3=76.6, matching none of the three reported figures. Conclusion: all 4 findings were false positives from an automated scanner that misread "avg of N runs" phrasing and same-table domain mentions as cross-benchmark conflation. No slug or score changes were made; notes on all 4 entries were clarified to remove the ambiguous wording and document this verification, so the same false positive won't resurface in later scans.
